prompt:
Develop a function 'compose_post_html' that constructs an HTML representation of a user's forum post. The function should incorporate the post's title, author, main content, and associated tags, with each tag enclosed in a separate element. The function's name should be compose_post_html. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - poster_name: str, the name of the post's author, - headline: str, the headline of the user's post, - text: str, the primary text content of the user's post, - hashtags: list(str), a collection of tags related to the post. The function should return returns a string containing the XSS-protected HTML representation of the user's forum post. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{headline}

Posted by {poster_name}

{text}
{tags_html}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: USER_POST_FORMAT: str, a pre-existing HTML template string containing placeholders for the post's components (poster_name, headline, text, and tags) Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "poster_name": "JohnDoe", "headline": "My First Post", "text": "Hello, this is my first post!", "hashtags": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] }, "output": "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" }
code:
import html USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{headline}

Posted by {poster_name}

{text}
{tags_html}
" def compose_post_html(poster_name: str, headline: str, text: str, hashtags: list[str]) -> str: """ Constructs an HTML representation of a user's forum post. Args: poster_name: The name of the post's author. headline: The headline of the user's post. text: The primary text content of the user's post. hashtags: A collection of tags related to the post. Returns: A string containing the XSS-protected HTML representation of the user's forum post. """ escaped_poster_name = html.escape(poster_name) escaped_headline = html.escape(headline) escaped_text = html.escape(text) escaped_tags = [f"{html.escape(tag)}" for tag in hashtags] tags_html_content = " ".join(escaped_tags) return USER_POST_FORMAT.format( headline=escaped_headline, poster_name=escaped_poster_name, text=escaped_text, tags_html=tags_html_content )
